topic_title: Reading from a file — open, read lines, close
verdict: PASS — all 6 checks clean. 2 soft findings (non-blocking): (1) sum() used in §7 Pattern 1 code example without prior entry in any topic's concepts_introduced — a built-in used alongside len() but not formally introduced; recommend either adding 'sum() built-in' to this topic's concepts_introduced or replacing with a manual addition loop in §7; (2) %%writefile in §9 Hands-On Exercise is a Jupyter/Colab cell magic, not Python — a zero-background learner may not know what it means; recommend adding a one-line parenthetical: '(this is a Colab shortcut to create a file — your instructor will show you this in the lab)'. Both findings are below the ≥3 hard-fail threshold.

notes: All 8 concepts_introduced are new to 12.6. File object, open(), read mode, .read(), .readlines(), .readline(), with block, FileNotFoundError all defined inline at first use. PASS.
.strip() and .split() referenced as Week 11 string methods — prior concept. PASS.
int() referenced as type conversion — Week 11 prior concept. PASS.
TypeError referenced in §8 best-practices table — in 12.5 concepts_introduced. PASS.
split('=', 1) two-argument form used in §7 Pattern 2 — explained inline ('splits at the first = only'). PASS.
Write/append modes ('w', 'a') appear in §5.2 table — explicitly deferred to Topic 12.7. PASS per scope_discipline.
FileNotFoundError: introduced inline; try/except handling deferred to 'a later topic'. PASS.
Binary files: 'not covered here' explicit defer. PASS.

sum() in §7 Pattern 1: NOT in any prior concepts_introduced. Used alongside len() in a code comment. Flagged as beginner_readability soft finding (undefined_terms), not sequence_drift hard fail, because it is used as a named built-in in a practical context without being taught.
%%writefile in §9: Jupyter cell magic, not Python. Flagged as beginner_readability soft finding.

topic_scope.notes: All sections stay within 'reading from a file'. Write/append modes named in table but deferred. FileNotFoundError introduced inline as direct consequence of open() with non-existent file — on-headline. try/except deferred. Binary files deferred. CSV module not mentioned. No scope_creep detected.

findings.location: §7 Real-World Patterns — Pattern 1 code example: print('Average:', sum(scores) / len(scores))
findings.detail: sum() is used as a built-in alongside len() but has no prior entry in any topic's concepts_introduced. For a strict zero-background learner, it is undefined. Recommend either: (a) adding 'sum() built-in' to this topic's concepts_introduced and defining it inline ('sum(list) adds up all the numbers in a list'); or (b) replacing with a manual accumulator loop that uses only taught concepts.

%%writefile is a Jupyter/Colab cell magic command, not Python. A zero-background learner has no context for this syntax. Recommend adding a parenthetical: '(this is a Colab shortcut that creates a file — your instructor will demonstrate it in the lab)' or replacing with explicit Python write-mode instructions.

beginner_readability.note: 2 soft findings — below the ≥3 hard-fail threshold. Register is plain, active, 'you'-addressed throughout. All new terms bold-defined on first use. with block broken down line by line. No jargon dumps detected.
